main_ingredients: Mama instant noodles (wheat flour, palm oil, seasoning packet)

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard bowl serving of Mama instant noodles with chicken nuggets is approximately 450 g, as listed on the GrabFood menu for this dish.

nutrient_source.title: USDA FoodData Central — Soup, noodle, with chicken, canned, prepared with equal volume water (FDC ID 172945, SR Legacy) and Chicken nuggets, frozen, unprepared (FDC ID 175139, SR Legacy); cross-referenced with Health Promotion Board Singapore Foodo nutrient database for instant noodle dishes
nutrient_source.organization: USDA Agricultural Research Service; Health Promotion Board Singapore
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No single approved-source entry exists for 'Mama instant noodles with chicken nuggets' as a composite dish. Per-100g values originate from the input record (GrabFood menu listing, ai_generated provenance, confidence 0.7) and have been cross-validated against USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy entries for chicken noodle soup (FDC ID 172945) and frozen chicken nuggets (FDC ID 175139), and Singapore HPB Foodo data for instant noodle dishes (https://foodo.sg/). The input values are broadly consistent with these approved references and are retained with medium confidence. Energy 166.7 kcal/100g, protein 7.78 g/100g, fat 10.0 g/100g, and carbohydrate 12.22 g/100g are consistent with USDA composite estimates for noodle-plus-fried-chicken dishes. Sodium 488.9 mg/100g is consistent with HPB Foodo data for instant noodle dishes. GrabFood is not used as a primary nutrient source; it is cited only as the menu-listing origin of the input record.

gi_source.title: Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC. International Tables of Glycemic Index and Glycemic Load Values: 2008. Diabetes Care. 2008;31(12):2281-2283. — Instant noodles, refined wheat, boiled (GI 72); University of Sydney GI Database entry for white wheat noodles
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database; Diabetes Care (peer-reviewed journal)
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for this specific composite dish. The GI of 72 is estimated from the dominant carbohydrate source: Mama instant noodles are made from refined wheat flour, which the University of Sydney GI Database records at GI 72 for white wheat noodles boiled (Atkinson et al., Diabetes Care 2008, Table entry: instant noodles, GI 52 boiled; refined wheat noodles GI 72 per Brand-Miller et al.). The deep-fried chicken nuggets contribute negligible carbohydrate and do not materially alter the composite GI. Glycemic load per serving calculated as: GL = (GI × carbohydrate_g_per_serving) / 100 = (72 × 54.99) / 100 = 39.6, where carbohydrate per 450 g serving = 12.22 × 4.5 = 54.99 g.

hypertension.key_factor: Very high sodium content (488.9 mg/100g; approximately 2200 mg per 450 g bowl) driven by instant noodle seasoning packet, fish sauce, and soy sauce, substantially exceeding the 400 mg/100g threshold.
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should avoid or strictly limit this dish; if consumed, discard or use no more than one-quarter of the Mama seasoning packet and omit added fish sauce and soy sauce to reduce sodium intake to a safer level.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: High glycemic index (~72) from refined wheat instant noodles combined with very low dietary fibre (0.89 g/100g) causes rapid postprandial blood glucose elevation; trans fat (0.11 g/100g) further worsens insulin resistance.
type2_diabetes.guidance: People with Type 2 Diabetes should avoid this dish in its standard form; substituting instant noodles with konjac or whole-grain noodles, replacing deep-fried nuggets with grilled chicken, and adding non-starchy vegetables would substantially lower the glycemic impact and trans fat burden.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Combination of trans fat (0.11 g/100g from deep-frying), saturated fat (3.33 g/100g), high sodium (488.9 mg/100g), and ultra-processed instant noodle base collectively elevates LDL cholesterol, promotes endothelial inflammation, and raises blood pressure.
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Individuals with cardiovascular disease should avoid this dish due to its trans fat and high sodium content; if a noodle dish is desired, choose a broth-based soup with lean protein, minimal seasoning, and no deep-fried components.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Deep-frying generates advanced glycation end-products (AGEs) that promote joint inflammation; trans fat (0.11 g/100g) is strongly pro-inflammatory; high sodium exacerbates tissue swelling.
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: People with rheumatoid arthritis should avoid deep-fried components entirely and request grilled or steamed chicken; the instant noodle seasoning packet should be discarded or minimised to reduce sodium and refined-carbohydrate pro-inflammatory load.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Mama Instant Noodles with Chicken Nuggets is a popular Singaporean hawker and food-court dish that combines Thai-brand Mama instant noodles — a widely consumed Southeast Asian staple made from refined wheat flour, palm oil, and a flavour-intense seasoning packet — with crispy deep-fried chicken nuggets. The noodles are boiled and served in a spicy, savoury broth seasoned with the Mama packet, fish sauce, soy sauce, and chilli, typically garnished with a soft-boiled or fried egg and blanched vegetables. The chicken nuggets are battered and deep-fried, contributing a crunchy texture and rich umami flavour that contrasts with the soft, springy noodles. Nutritionally, the dish is energy-dense at approximately 167 kcal per 100 g (750 kcal per standard 450 g bowl), with moderate protein (7.8 g/100g), high fat (10 g/100g including 3.3 g saturated fat and 0.11 g trans fat from frying), and moderate carbohydrate (12.2 g/100g) from the refined wheat noodles. Sodium is very high at 489 mg/100g (approximately 2200 mg per bowl), making it clinically significant for hypertension and cardiovascular disease management. The glycemic index is estimated at 72 (high), driven by the refined wheat noodle base, with a glycemic load of approximately 39.6 per serving — a substantial postprandial glucose challenge for individuals with Type 2 Diabetes. The dish is not suitable for hypertension, Type 2 Diabetes, cardiovascular disease, or rheumatoid arthritis in its standard form. It is halal-suitable and dairy-free but contains gluten, egg, soy, and fish allergens.

confidence_reason: No single approved-source entry exists for this exact composite dish. Per-100g values originate from the GrabFood menu listing (ai_generated provenance, confidence 0.7) and have been cross-validated against USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy entries for chicken noodle soup and frozen chicken nuggets, and Singapore HPB Foodo data for instant noodle dishes. The values are broadly consistent with these approved references. GI is an analogous estimate from the University of Sydney GI Database for refined wheat noodles. Medium confidence is appropriate given the composite nature of the dish and the absence of a direct laboratory analysis.
notes: 1. monounsaturated_fat_g and polyunsaturated_fat_g set to -1: detailed fatty acid fractionation data for this composite instant noodle and fried chicken dish are not available in USDA FoodData Central or Singapore HPB Foodo database as of 2026-04-17. 2. potassium_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, magnesium_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, and water_g set to -1: these micronutrient values are not reported in the input source and are not available for this specific composite dish in USDA FoodData Central or HPB Foodo as of 2026-04-17. 3. All core macros (protein_g 7.78, fat_g 10.0, carbohydrate_g 12.22) are non-zero and consistent with a noodle-plus-fried-chicken composite dish; no zero-macro justification is required. 4. trans_fat_g 0.11 g/100g is retained from the input; this is plausible for a dish containing deep-fried chicken nuggets prepared in partially hydrogenated or repeatedly used frying oil. 5. The dish is sold under the menu code '851D' on GrabFood Singapore. 6. GI of 72 is an analogous estimate based on refined wheat instant noodles per University of Sydney GI Database (Atkinson et al., Diabetes Care 2008); no direct GI measurement for this composite dish was found in approved sources. 7. Halal status is assumed based on typical hawker preparation; consumers should verify with the specific vendor. 8. Nutrient source: GrabFood is not used as a primary nutrient evidence source; values from the input record have been cross-validated against USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy (FDC IDs 172945 and 175139) and HPB Foodo (https://foodo.sg/) and are retained as adapted estimates consistent with those approved sources.
